name: Chad Bianco
background: Sheriff of Riverside County, leading one of the largest law enforcement agencies in California. Husband and father.
statement: My name is Chad Bianco. I'm a husband, a father, and the Sheriff of Riverside County. I didn't rise through politics. I rose through public service. As Sheriff, I lead one of the largest law enforcement agencies in California. We've reduced violent crime, stood up for victims, and supported the men and women who protect our communities. I've made hard decisions under pressure, in situations with real consequences. Leadership isn't about slogans. When common sense fails, regular people pay the price. California is at a turning point. Too many families are struggling just to get by. Moms and dads are working longer hours and still falling behind. Jobs are leaving, pushing our children to look elsewhere for opportunity. Buying a home feels out of reach. Gas, groceries, and utilities cost more every month. People are doing everything right and still getting squeezed. As Governor, I will bring accountability and common sense back to state government. I will cut through bureaucracy that keeps families from rebuilding after wildfires. I will demand real results for the billions spent on homelessness, and end the cycle of spending without accountability. We may not agree on everything, but we all want the same basics: safe neighborhoods, opportunity for our kids, and a fair shot at getting ahead.

positions.economy: Bring jobs back to California; reduce cost of living (gas, groceries, utilities)
positions.housing: Make home-buying accessible; cut bureaucracy blocking wildfire rebuilding
positions.publicSafety: Reduce violent crime; accountability in law enforcement; end homelessness spending without results
positions.government: Accountability and common sense in state government; end wasteful spending

contact.address: 3649 Mission Inn Ave., Floor 2, Riverside, CA 92501
